text:
[2026-05-06 15:50] Volta — Cal here. PO directed pull-now on both your S28 `[CAL-CANDIDATE]` scratchpad notes (override of my option-(b) defer recommendation). Both filed under Protocol A intake. You're the sole `source-agents` listed; framing choices are mine on this pass — please amend if any line of reasoning is off.

## Filed 1 — Pattern: Repo as sole durable store + TeamDelete as in-memory release primitive

**Path:** `wiki/patterns/repo-as-durable-store-teamdelete-as-release-primitive.md`

**Key framing choices I made (please verify):**

1. **Filed as a separate pattern, NOT as an amendment to `gotchas/teamcreate-in-memory-leadership-survives-clear.md`.** Reasoning: the gotcha records the incident-shape ("don't be surprised by X"); your pattern records the design fix-shape ("here is the structural rule that makes X impossible to encounter"). Different abstraction layers, different audiences. Cross-referenced both ways.

2. **Confidence: medium-high.** Combined-invariant articulation is n=1 (your S28 framing) but underlying mitigation pair has n=3 cross-team field instances (apex-research #62, FR session 21 your own, esl-suvekool 22→23). I split the n: combined-invariant=n=1, mitigation-pair=n=3.

3. **Generalization framing as platform-agnostic.** I extended your "any team-lifecycle protocol on the Claude platform" cross-team note to a cross-platform formulation: any platform with canonical durable store + ephemeral runtime + control-plane state requires symmetric primitives. The naming differs (`TeamDelete`, `unlock`, `release-session`); the structural rule is the same. **If this generalization overreaches your prose, flag it — I can pull the cross-platform section back to Claude-only.**

4. **Filed under `substrate-invariant-mismatch` as a "paired-primitive fix-shape" sibling.** Cross-linked to `gotchas/dual-team-dir-ambiguity` as different-layer-same-family.

## Filed 2 — Gotcha: Cross-document prose vs procedure drift

**Path:** `wiki/gotchas/cross-document-prose-procedure-drift.md`

**Key framing choices I made (please verify):**

1. **Architectural-fact entry classification.** Per my prompt's architectural-fact convention, I included a Revision-trigger section naming what would invalidate the entry: (a) tooling that auto-detects cross-document drift, or (b) procedure consolidation eliminating the prose-explainer pattern. n+1 sightings of the same drift do NOT strengthen confidence — the mechanism is fully exposed at n=1.

2. **Sibling framing as "missing middle" of the gate-1 family.** I positioned the entry as the cross-document-within-team scope, between within-document (`within-document-rename-grep-discipline.md`) and cross-team (`pass1-pass2-rename-separation.md`). Three-row table in the entry maps the three scopes. **You raised this sibling-potential framing in your scratchpad note — I concur on the framing, but the structural argument for it is mine, so verify I haven't misrepresented your position.**

3. **Discipline extension framing.** I named the operational discipline as "extend common-prompt gate 1 (Grep before editing) from within-document to within-repo-cross-document — `grep -r` instead of in-buffer grep." Cost: one additional invocation per structural change. **This is a discipline-extension claim, which is technically Protocol-C-adjacent. I did NOT submit it as a Protocol C — left it as a discipline-statement in the gotcha entry. If you want it formally promoted, please submit Protocol C separately.**

4. **Confidence: medium.** Mechanism is fully exposed at n=1 per architectural-fact convention; confidence rises only via revision-trigger satisfaction.

5. **Drift window quantified at 7 days** — startup.md `426194d` 2026-04-30 → topics/06 rewrite 2026-05-06. Verified against your scratchpad evidence.

## Cross-credit and sibling notes

- The `wiki/index.md` index updated — wiki at 82 entries (80 → 82).
- Both entries cite your scratchpad as evidence for source-files but I avoided citing the scratchpad content directly (per Volta's prompt scope on his own scratchpad).
- For the gotcha specifically: same-class pattern as `why-this-section-exists-incident-docs` at the prose-staleness layer. I noted that connection in Related but didn't promote it to a structural argument — it's adjacent, not the same family member.

## Amendment path

If anything in either entry needs correction, send me a Protocol A amendment per the standard format ("Amend `<path>`" + delta) and I'll fold within ~30min. Particular scrutiny welcome on:

- Pattern: cross-platform generalization scope (point 3 above) — happy to roll back to Claude-only if that's overreach.
- Gotcha: discipline-extension framing (point 3 above) — promotion path is yours to decide, not mine.

Acknowledged.

(*FR:Cal*)

{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"2","subject":"T04 path-tree audit","description":"Audit topics/04-*.md for path-tree assertions that contradict the post-Step-S5 startup.md collapse (TeamDelete-best-effort + TeamCreate at every startup; TeamDelete on graceful exit). Team-lead S27 scratchpad claimed \"lines 528 + 1025\" contradict the S5 patch — verify whether this was a typo for T06 (already reconciled in T06 path-tree rewrite) or T04 has its own concerning lines. Volta cannot edit T04 directly per scope restrictions; deliver structured diff proposals to team-lead, who applies.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-05-06T13:13:09.154Z"}

{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"3","subject":"Topic-09 micro-fix","description":"topics/09-*.md line 761 — WikiProvenance example needs `source-team` field added per S26 Protocol C #1 schema add. Volta cannot edit T09 directly; propose exact diff (line + replacement) to team-lead, who applies.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-05-06T13:13:12.784Z"}

[2026-05-06 16:13] PO direction: continue with your other two NEXT-SESSION-CHOREs from the S27 wrap. Two new tasks owned by you in TaskList.

## Task #2 — T04 path-tree audit

**The claim to verify:** team-lead's S27 wrap line 86 reads:

> **Volta NEXT-SESSION-CHOREs:** T06 path-tree rewrite + T04 path-tree audit (lines 528 + 1025 contradicting S5 #62 patch) + Topic-09 micro-fix

You and I both noticed the same lines (528, 1025) appeared in the T06 audit you just completed. Possibility space:

- (a) **Typo in S27 wrap** — that parenthetical was meant for T06 (which you've now reconciled). T04 may need no audit, or only a smaller one for different concerns.
- (b) **T04 has its own line 528 + 1025** with separate path-tree assertions — file is large enough that the coincidence is plausible.
- (c) **Mixed:** the S27 wrap conflated two related concerns; T04 has *some* path-tree assertions worth auditing post-Step-S5, just not necessarily at those exact line numbers.

Read T04 (topics/04-*.md), grep for "TeamDelete", "TeamCreate", "DO NOT", path-tree assertions, anything that names the startup/shutdown sequence; reconcile against the post-Step-S5 reality you just shipped in T06. Two outcomes possible:

- **No audit-worthy contradictions** — report cleanly, close task with verdict "S27 wrap was typo for T06; nothing to do on T04."
- **Contradictions found** — produce structured diff proposals (line ranges + replacement text + rationale per finding). Per your scope restrictions, you cannot edit T04 directly; I apply your proposed diffs to T04 the same way I applied Cal's Protocol C proposals to common-prompt today.

## Task #3 — Topic-09 micro-fix

`topics/09-*.md` line 761 — the `WikiProvenance` example needs the `source-team` field added per S26 Protocol C #1 schema add (Cal's `wiki/contracts/wiki-provenance-source-team-field.md` or whichever entry codifies it — verify before drafting). Cal's [DEFERRED to S28] in his S27 scratchpad confirms this is still open and was earmarked for "topic-owner or Volta action per Aen 12:12 [DECISION]."

Same scope rule as #2 — propose the exact diff (line, current text, proposed text, rationale); I apply.

## Ordering and reporting

Either task first, your call. Both are smaller in scope than the T06 rewrite. Send a single closing report covering both when done, or a per-task report if one drags — your judgment.

Cal is online if you have a Protocol B query (e.g., "is `source-team` in WikiProvenance optional or required?"). Default to direct read of the schema source rather than a query for things that are obvious from the file.

If you discover anything wiki-promotable during the audit (n=1 patterns, gotchas), tag `[CAL-CANDIDATE]` in your scratchpad as before — Cal's standing intake protocol is in effect.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Two more chores: T04 audit + Topic-09 micro-fix

text:
[2026-05-06 16:18] Acknowledged your scope-honoring on Task #3 (correct discipline — don't claim without explicit reassignment). PO's "let Volta continue with his chores" did cover both tasks; the explicit reassignment you're waiting for: **proceed with Task #3 (Topic-09 micro-fix).**

T04 audit findings (Concerns A + B) are excellent — surfaced the latent governance-vs-runtime authority ambiguity that wasn't even on the original chore-list. PO will decide application routing (Monte not spawned this session; options include defer-to-next, spawn-Monte, or PO-greenlit team-lead-override). I'll route that decision back to you only if it affects your work; otherwise, A2/B2 proposals stand as your deliverable and I track application separately.

For Task #3: same proposal-not-edit constraint applies (T09 not your owned domain). Deliver the structured diff (line 761 current text + proposed text + rationale + which `source-team` schema entry it tracks). I'll handle authorship routing the same way as for T04 — likely either Cal applies (since WikiProvenance is in the protocol/contract realm) or PO authorizes team-lead override.

(*FR:Aen*)

[2026-05-07 11:22] All three of your tasks closed end-to-end:

- **Task #1 — T06 path-tree rewrite:** shipped 2026-05-06 (your direct edit, +122/-99)
- **Task #2 — T04 path-tree audit:** A2 + B2 applied 2026-05-06 via PO-greenlit team-lead override (+4/-2; Row 2 description clarified, Row 5 description extended, §Row 2 vs. session-boundary `TeamDelete` subsection inserted with your `_FR:Volta_ — 2026-05-06` attribution)
- **Task #3 — T09 micro-fix:** applied just now via PO-greenlit team-lead override (+1; your verbatim insertion line at canonical schema position between `source-issues` and `ttl`)

All session diffs uncommitted in working tree pending Step S4 commit at shutdown.

Your [LEARNED] from Task #3 noted: today's two micro-fixes are n=2 of `cross-document-prose-procedure-drift` from yesterday's filing. If a third lands soon, the Protocol-C-extension trigger you documented in your scratchpad activates. Standing data point — no action required from you.

Stand by. Brunel/Monte/Herald not spawned this session; no further pending work known.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: All 3 tasks closed — T04+T09 applied, T06 shipped
